competencies.competency_text: Describes relationships (including between humans and animals/nature) and traditions (art forms, celebrations, festivals) in the family and community
competencies.justification: Sections 'A Special Day in School' (Republic Day and Independence Day described as school-community celebrations), 'Our Vibrant Culture' (dance forms, drumming traditions), and 'Diversity Everywhere' (headgear as regional tradition in weddings and formal occasions) all compel students to describe art forms, celebrations, and cultural traditions.

competencies.competency_text: Describes the relationship between the natural environment and cultural practices in their immediate environment (nature of work, food, festivals, traditions)
competencies.justification: Section 'Diversity Everywhere' uses the forest-garden metaphor to explicitly connect natural diversity to cultural strength, and links regional headgear to geography and climate. National symbols (tiger, peacock) in 'Symbols that Speak' connect natural fauna to cultural identity and pride.

competencies.competency_text: Observes and describes cultural diversity in their immediate environment (food, clothing, games, different seasons, festivals related to harvest and sowing)
competencies.justification: The chapter's core architecture requires observing and documenting cultural diversity: Activity 5 ('Our Vibrant Culture') surveys languages in class and documents the word 'mango' across languages; 'Diversity Everywhere' describes headgear from Rajasthan and Himachal Pradesh and dance forms from different states; the Cultural Fair Project ('Spirit of Togetherness') requires each group to research traditional dress, food, festivals, and languages of an assigned state.

competencies.competency_text: Learns about basic social and behavioural norms, values, and dispositions that benefit our social and natural environments and that help our society function smoothly (using dustbins, standing in queues, conserving water, using public transportation, keeping one's environment clean, always helping others in need regardless of background)
competencies.justification: Section 'Finding India in Currency Notes' architecturally teaches the Swachh Bharat Abhiyan cleanliness norm (the spectacles symbol on the currency note, Gandhi ji's ashram cleanliness rules). Section 'A Special Day in School' presents India's Constitution as the book of rules that keeps the country safe and functioning smoothly — a direct engagement with social norms and civic values.

competencies.competency_text: Reads simple maps of city, state, and country to identify natural and humanmade features (well, lake, post office, school, hospital) with reference to symbols and directions
competencies.justification: Activity 7 in 'Diversity Everywhere' explicitly requires students to identify and mark the states of origin of classical dance forms on a map of India — engaging map-reading at the country level to identify humanmade/political features (states).

competencies.competency_text: Performs simple inquiry related to specific questions independently or in groups
competencies.justification: Three sections compel inquiry: 'Find out' in 'Symbols that Speak' (research state symbols with teacher); Activity 5 in 'Our Vibrant Culture' (find out languages spoken by classmates); and the Cultural Fair Project in 'Spirit of Togetherness' (group inquiry into an assigned state covering dress, food, festivals, languages, and dance).

competencies.competency_text: Presents observations and findings through different creative modes (drawing, diagram, poem, play, skit, oral and written expression)
competencies.justification: Multiple sections require creative presentation: Activity 1 ('A Special Day in School') — poster or model of state tableau; Draw task in 'Symbols that Speak' — draw school symbol with written caption; Activity 4 ('Finding India in Currency Notes') — design a school emblem; Cultural Fair Project ('Spirit of Togetherness') — prepare presentations, posters, and cutouts; group oral discussion at the fair.
